name: Cordel de la Solana Trail
name_original: Cañada de los Arquillos y Cordel de la Solana – trail running circular desde Alboloduy (PR-A 16)
description: Jog the difficult 7.7-mile Cordel de la Solana Trail in Sierra Nevada National Park, featuring diverse terrain and scenic views.
region_intro: Jogging around Alboloduy offers routes through varied terrain, characterized by significant elevation changes and natural landscapes. The region features paths that ascend into hills, providing panoramic views of the surrounding area. Running routes often traverse rural settings, connecting small towns and offering a blend of challenging climbs and scenic stretches. Best jogging routes around Alboloduy The most popular jogging route is Cordel de la Solana Trail, a 7.7 miles (12.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This route features notable elevation gain, making it a challenging option for runners. Another top favourite among local runners is Alicún-Huécija Viewpoint loop from Alicún, a difficult 9.9 miles (16.0 km) path. This running route includes a viewpoint, offering expansive vistas of the landscape. Local runners also love the Balsa de las Perillas loop from Canjáyar, a 12.8 miles (20.6 km) trail leading through hilly terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Jogging around Alboloduy is defined by hilly terrain, scenic viewpoints, and challenging ascents. The network offers options for various ability levels, though many routes feature significant elevation changes. The routes in Alboloduy are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 50 runners have used komoot to explore Alboloduy's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes around Alboloduy?
A: Jogging around Alboloduy is characterized by &lt;strong&gt;hilly terrain&lt;/strong&gt; and significant elevation changes. Routes often traverse rural settings, connecting small towns and offering a blend of challenging climbs and scenic stretches through natural landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Alboloduy?
A: While Alboloduy's running routes are generally known for their challenging, hilly terrain, there is one route classified as moderate. However, most of the routes feature significant elevation changes, making them more suitable for experienced runners.

Q: What are some challenging long-distance running trails in the Alboloduy area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa de las Perillas loop from Canjáyar&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 12.8 miles (20.6 km) trail with substantial elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24519492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Alhama de Almería&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 9.5 miles (15.3 km) with considerable ascent.

Q: How many running routes are available in Alboloduy?
A: There are approximately 6 dedicated running routes available in Alboloduy, offering a variety of distances and challenges for runners.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Alboloduy?
A: The running routes in Alboloduy are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the challenging ascents, scenic viewpoints, and the opportunity to explore the varied natural terrain.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Alboloduy?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Alboloduy are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24520009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alicún-Huécija Viewpoint loop from Alicún&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option that includes a scenic viewpoint.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see along the running routes?
A: While running, you might encounter natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6042540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rambla de los Yesos&lt;/a&gt; or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desert trails&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass near charming settlements such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301536" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alboloduy Village&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Íllar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints on the running trails in Alboloduy?
A: Yes, several routes offer expansive vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24520009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alicún-Huécija Viewpoint loop from Alicún&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights a viewpoint, providing panoramic views of the landscape. You can also find general &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6058083" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful view&lt;/a&gt; highlights in the area.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Alboloduy?
A: Alboloduy's climate can be quite warm, especially in summer. Spring and autumn generally offer more moderate temperatures, making them ideal for running. Winter can also be pleasant, though it's advisable to check local weather forecasts for cooler conditions.

Q: How can I find parking or public transport options for the running trails?
A: Given the rural nature of Alboloduy, accessing trailheads often requires personal transport. Parking availability can vary by starting point, so it's recommended to check specific route details or local information for designated parking areas. Public transport options are generally limited in this region.

Q: Are there any hidden gem running routes in Alboloduy?
A: While the komoot community highly rates the main routes, exploring less-trafficked paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Ohanes&lt;/a&gt; can offer a sense of discovery. These routes often provide a more secluded experience amidst the natural desert and hilly landscapes.
tips: Place of great geological diversity whose geological materials correspond to the metamorphic of the nevadofilábride and the sedimentary of the so-called alpujárride. In this ravine, or boulevard, we can see small "cerradas" made of conglomerates and clay.
Cordel from the foothills of the Sierra Nevada Almeriense, at its easternmost end. It is located in the Comarca del Medio Andarax, sub-region of Bajo Nacimiento.

This town, on the right bank of the Nacimiento River, belongs to the Sierra Nevada Natural Park. The cultivation of the Ohanes grape was very important here during the 19th century, which, after successive droughts in the 20th century, was lost and was replaced by the cultivation of other fruit trees and orchards.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Cordel de la Solana Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route that starts and ends in the town of Alboloduy. You can typically find parking within the village itself, which serves as the trailhead for this route.

faqs.question: Is the Cordel de la Solana Trail suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: While generally considered to have a low to medium difficulty, the trail does feature continuous small ascents and descents, soft sand sections in the ramblas, and some abrupt climbs. It's manageable for most people with good fitness, but beginners should be prepared for varied terrain and elevation changes.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a diverse terrain including traditional paths, sections along the Nacimiento river, and stretches through pine groves. There are also parts that follow dry riverbeds (ramblas) like the Rambla de los Yesos, where the ground can be soft and sandy. Expect forest tracks and more rugged footpaths (veredas), with continuous undulations and some significant descents.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers breathtaking views of the Sierra Nevada mountains and the Andarax Valley. You'll pass by the Cañada de los Arquillos, the Cordel de la Solana, and the Alboloduy Village and Church. Look out for unique geological formations like "Chimeneas de las Hadas" (fairy chimneys) in the ramblas, a small waterfall, and agricultural areas with fruit and olive trees.

faqs.question: Is the Cordel de la Solana Trail a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Cordel de la Solana Trail is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same location, Alboloduy, making navigation straightforward.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog the Cordel de la Solana Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in Andalusia, which can get very hot in summer. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging, with comfortable weather and vibrant scenery. Winter can also be a good option, though some higher sections might be cooler.

faqs.question: Is the Cordel de la Solana Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within the Sierra Nevada National Park and Nature Reserve, dogs are generally permitted but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Cordel de la Solana Trail itself, as it's a public trail within the Sierra Nevada National Park and Nature Reserve.

faqs.question: What regulations should I be aware of when jogging in Sierra Nevada National Park?
faqs.answer: When jogging within the Sierra Nevada National Park, it's important to stay on marked trails, avoid disturbing wildlife or plants, and carry out all your trash. Camping is restricted, and fires are generally prohibited. Always respect local signage and regulations to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route primarily follows the Cordel de la Solana for about 94% of its length. It also briefly intersects with sections of the Camino Mozárabe. Almería-Granada, Vereda de Alcozayar, and the Rambla de los Yesos.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Alboloduy, a village where you can typically find basic amenities such as cafes or small shops. For more extensive options, you might need to visit larger towns in the surrounding Andarax Valley.
